sections.paragraph:
Βενιαμ(ε)ίν, ὁ indecl. (בִּנְיָמִין.—LXX; JosAs [Βενιαμήν]; AscIs, Philo, Test12Patr. On spelling B-D-F §38.—In Joseph. βενιαμ(ε)ίς, gen. εῖ [Ant. 2, 122]) Benjamin, Israelite tribe (K-DSchunck, Benjamin. Untersuchungen zur Entstehung u. Gesch. eines israel. Stammes ’63) Ac 13:21; Ro 11:1; Phil 3:5; Rv 7:8.

indecl. indecl. = indeclinable

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

AscIs AscIs = Ascension of Isaiah—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Test12Patr Test12Patr = Testaments of the Twelve Patriarchs, with interpolations II b.c.–III a.d.—List 2

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

gen. gen. = genitive

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 174
